channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 52 replies — 208.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:57 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62344894
>>62339328
if you didn't sell it when it was over 100k it was not the right move

62345001
>>62344890
Anon, if you believe in BTC long term, buying is the easy part. Holding is harder. And now there are even ways to put BTC to work through native staking without giving up self-custody. Do with that information what you will.

62345004
>>62339462
What's the problem with that?

62345048
>>62344894
You can buy the dip ser

62345050
>>62341036
Buy eth and sol too

62345054
>>62340066
What's happening in October?

62345074
>>62345048
yea but you could have bought the 50% discount with some of the profits instead of fresh shekels

62345096
>>62345074
Smart guy

62345100
>>62345001
How can we stake btc?

62345127
hold till $1M

62345299
>>62345054
were supposed to bottom, 4 year cycle or some shit.

62345600
>>62345100
You haven't heard of Babylon? Native BTC staking already exists there. DYOR anon.

62345604
>>62339510
The real challenge is whether August actually lets you buy lower. Markets have a habit of humbling everyone's timelines.

62345772
>>62345299
Is 4year cycle even real?

62345775
>>62345127
40 years from now

62345776
>>62339328
I sold a small amount at 110k last year, bought a car. Unironically I felt bad once it started pumping back to 120k. Now my car has holded it's value better than BTC.

62345783
>>62345776
Car price depreciated. Nothing special

62345787
>>62341032
I just want x1000 . Utility is nothing

62345800
>>62345600
centralised services doesn't really give me a good impression

62345806
When to sell?

62345827
>>62339328
>Btc
>Eth
>Hbar
>Neo
>Etc
>Kava
>Rose
>Inj

62346229
>>62344890
Yep. Make sure you ignore the fiat value. If it falls to zero, BTC is a new way of finance independent from the outdated monetary system. Be grateful you are part of the revolution. I too will soon enter BTC paradise. Any day now...

62346754
>>62345800
Anon, where did I say anything about centralized services? You're arguing against something I never said. Read up on their TBVs and maybe it'll click.

62346765
>>62339730
That's understandable. It's hard to judge past decisions based on outcomes we couldn't have known at the time.

62349025
>>62346229
Btc will never go to zero. Stop smoking

62349066
>>62345827
oldie bags but gold . Hodl

62349435
>>62346754
Onchain is the game

62349439
>>62345806
Depends on you lmao

62349795
>>62339328
If I had sold to pay off debt, buy a house, or improve my life, I wouldn't regret it. You can't eat unrealized gains.

62350085
>>62345787
go for shitcoins like memecoins on solana

item.content:encoded:

FREE SBF FREE HIM HE DINDU NUFFIN

>>62340556

i want him released to dab on cryptopoors

what an insufferable parasite.

62340607

He years to pin the weasel once again

62340667
>>62340556
SBF was a scapegoat who got the book thrown at him because he was the public face of his company, that frumpy bitch who worked with him was just as guilty and got a slap on the wrist.

He deserves a pardon.

>>62340667
Buy an ad

62340751
He was a good yid that made an honest mistake

62340757
>>62340556

62340774
He did nothing wrong

62340784
I really hope he does get his pardon. Not because I'm basically a genetic relative of his being a mixed-breed half White half ashkenazi shitskin, but because it will open the eyes of boomers to Trump's blatant corruption. Everyone distinctly remembers this pudgy jew was the largest Democratic donor at the time who fiercely (lol) opposed Trump.

Will Trump save his fellow jew?

wen sbf gets his pardon /biz/ will be back and better than ever

62340800
>>62340784
Fucking LOL at the brown ID

62340809

i love her

62340822

62340847
>>62340556
he was a good boy!

>>62340822
he won't be out till he's 57, unless Trump steps in.

Didn't he donate billions to Biden? Why would Trump want to free him?

62340902
>>62340809
Caroline where are you
I bumped your thread did you see

62340940
wait, you can apply for pardon now? how much is the application fee

62342311
>>62340809

62343676
>>62340940
here at TrumpPardonsInc, flexa-bill-itty is our MO. Installments, shiny rocks, firstborn (if cute), Klarna - we take it all. But cash under the table, in large bundles of used notes, generally preferred. Cash today and you can fly away!

62343685

62343716
>>62340556
SBF is a JEW

62344828
>>62340556
it's like watching a cockroach twitch after you spray it

62345305
>>62343685
>>62340809
She is a billionaire right now. Will you be her slave pet?

62345358
>>62340556
HE A GOOD BOY

62345450
the most retarded thing trump could do here is pardon sbf so he most certainly will do it

but hey if it gets us the next bull run...

>>62345305
she forfeited her money as part of the plea deal

62345466
>>62340556
He'll get it too. This administration has tirned into a fucking graft machine.

62349805
>>62340556
no bull run without sbf. I hope he gets freed

62349903
>>62340556
I want him to be free so that people can seethe irl. Let the goym deal in the real world.

62349982
>>62340607

62350024

item.title: >Iran completely shuts the strait >BTC goes up >Oil goes down Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when i… — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Iran completely shuts the strait >BTC goes up >Oil goes down Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when i…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:55 UTC

OP

>Iran completely shuts the strait
>BTC goes up
>Oil goes down
Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when it reacted negatively the first time this happened?

Early replies (first 3)

62349904
>>62349889
This is what broken markets do, the head of the Chicago CME warned about this. It's all fun and games for a while, until it breaks into a biblical catastrophe.
Which will happen end of summer.

62349914
The market is pricing in peace and a quick resolution to this small excursion.

62349976
>>62349904
We (yes, we) allowed a bunch of mentally ill hoarders, lolcows even, into power. Naturally, they made clown policies, which clown the markets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349979
>>62349976
Solution: Denying power. No more politburo commies, no more billionaires, no more politicians, behind seven layers of private scurity. Direct control and accountability for everyone.

62350027
>>62349979
well that will never happen

62350047
>>62349914

>Beginning of month 4 of quick resolution to this small excusion
>25 million dollar Apache helicopter destroyed by cheap drone with a lawnmower engine
>Hormuz is still closed

62350070
>>62350047
Americans deserve this for failing the emotional IQ test

62350079
>>62350070
*failing the emotional IQ test twice

item.title: BTC — 10 replies — 27.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:43 UTC

OP

Did anyone figure out why the value seems to be cyclical?
Whats the best exchange and wallet for a new to investing anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62347134
>>62347031
so they can continuously pump and dump and pretend it's simply "natural" and "mathematical" and whatever other bullshit marketing they do

62347139
>>62347031
>cycles
Human psychology

>exchange
Kraken

>wallet
Your memory, but I’ll let other people shill their products below

62347162
>>62347139
He's new to it. Start with the Bluewallet app on your phone, look for more secure alternatives as you go.

https://bitcoin.org/en/choose-your-wallet

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62347465
Thank you for your helpful responses frens

62347618
>>62347031
Its a self fulfilling prophecy at this point but it started because of the halving.

62347821
>>62347031
>Did anyone figure out why the value seems to be cyclical?
each 4 years there is a halving

>Whats the best exchange and wallet for a new to investing anon?
4chin censors the reply, probably site is run by some retards
b. core
change b. to full name of the coin
tripple check sources before install

source: og here

62347830
>>62347031

62348122
>>62347821
So the "b . Org" is the wrong one?

I hate how predatory search engines are now that we are the product

62350049
>>62348122
bitcoin core is the real program, og

bitcoin core dot org is the site
but also/better check github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin

62350055
>>62348122
>>62350049
bitcoin.org is the original site, yes, it has a list of wallets, including the bitcoincore main wallet

to get rich/avoid getting poor:
- keep backups
- doublecheck backups
- make more backups
- document how you restore and use backups, so if you give up on btc and find this back in 10 years you can recover it still
- more backups, in case of a fire

- do NOT keep money on exchange, withdraw
- do NOT move all money at once. go slow. send, wait hours, verify, continue
- just buy weekly or "daily" (daily cost averaging). buy more if it falls a lot
- never sell it all

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 33 replies — 25.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-11 07:48 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

62339778
Imagine caring this much, holy shit. Adult bully victim no doubt

62339781
>>62339778
Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.

62339829
>>62339778
idk bout that... xrp apparently lives rent free in linkbaggies minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339835
>>62339829
100%
And I caught him red handed

And Chainlinkies be all like: "Durrr imagine caring this isn't important"

62340175
>>62339844
Xrp hasn't reclaimed it's all time high after a whole decade. If xrp baggies didn't sell they have blown their chance of limiting their losses and buying something useful

Kaboom

62340182
>>62339768
Its biepum the yeet node operator
Give him a break, chainlink shilling and xrp fudding is his personality

62340345
>>62339768
so fxcking bullish

62340350
Boooomers and peelers won

62340365
>>62339768
>Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62340374
>>62339768
Why would anyone buy Chainlink or xrp I don't understand. Crypto is there to buy drugs online. Can you buy drugs online with Chainlink ? No. Can you buy drugs online with xrp? No. Case closed.

62340393
>>62339768
I don't hold link either tho
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62340516
everybody hates u dumb stinks

62340978
>>62339781
>words words words
It's funny how you're immediately identifiable by the spergy seethe that radiates from your posts.

Even funnier, >>62339844 posting the madmen meme.
>"We don't think about Linkies at all!!"
>Op literally creates a thread ranting about Chainlink because some other random thread that doesn't mention Chainlink could have been made by a Linkie

62340989
>>62340978
>TLDR linkjeet seethe

didnt read lol

62340992
BOOOOOOOOOOM

BULLISH AT XRP

62341232
Bumping this bullsih thread
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62342495
>>62339768
>My retarded shitposts made someone so mad he made an expose thread on me
FXCKING BULLISH KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62343674
>>62339768
>>62339781
>>62339835
>>62340989
>linkcucks and xrpaypigs having a retarded biz slapfight as they both go to 0

62344317
KABOOOOOOOOM>>62339768

62344795
>>62339781
This. I don't hold either but the link spam is so fucking stupid and annoying. They got decimated in a war length campaign of threads and saw zero wins and now it is over and over with this same shitty failed meme that ironically got funny when XRP posters just did it back to them to mock them. I have zero dog in this race but XRP is the clear winner in the messaging war.

62344908
>>62344795
>t. the same person a different id
manufacturing consensus on a dead board is pretty cringe desu senpai

62345267
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

62345354
Except XRP is actually being used

62347256
KABOOOOOOOOM

62347334
>>62344795
BOOOOOOOOOOM YOU HOLD XRP

62347434
>>62339781
>”I don’t care”
>paragraph about how much he cares

62347439
>>62339768
Both LINK and XRP are gay

62348331
I mean…it’s pretty hard not to cope and seethe with pictures like this

62348340
>>62348331
Must....not......boom...must...not.......
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
BULLISH AT THE TICKER XRP

62349668
>>62344908
You are incapable of conceiving of someone who just calls out your hypocrisy and annoying shilling. This is why you are how you are and you are like this because you do this. It is circular and self-feeding and swallowing. You are in a vortex, accusing me of doing exactly what you are in an even more retarded way than you are even accusing me of doing.
>>62347334
See the above reply. Same applies to you.

62349808
>>62349668
You are the verbose xrp shill though. You made this >>62349672 reply to the xrp general minutes after this one here in the same verbose style.

62349917
>>>62349808
Mentally ill VPN linkbaggie

item.title: my mom bumped into a neighbor on a street we use to live in back in the 90s her son was a literal chad, had 10/10 GF's,… — 14 replies — 22.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
my mom bumped into a neighbor on a street we use to live in back in the 90s her son was a literal chad, had 10/10 GF's,…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:28 UTC

OP

my mom bumped into a neighbor on a street we use to live in back in the 90s

her son was a literal chad, had 10/10 GF's, went to an elite private school, tall, handsome, etc

turns out he's now living in section 8 housing, addicted to online gambling and drugs

and all this time i imagined he had to have ended up as some super successful winner in life

Early replies (first 3)

62349865
you finally got your revenge anon. Ugly ass nerds victory, total chad death

62349886
>>62349860
"See? The Chad ended up in Section 8 housing and addicted to gambling and drugs. Therefore looks, popularity, and social success don't matter."
Hold your clown horses.
One guy crashing his life into a ditch doesn't magically prove that advantages aren't advantages. That's like seeing a former lottery winner go bankrupt and concluding money has no value. That's not an argument; that's a unicycle-powered statistical faceplant.
The guy started with a stack of advantages: attractiveness, social status, confidence, access to a good school, dating success, and likely a stronger social network than most people ever get. Those things are real advantages. They don't guarantee victory, but pretending they don't matter is pure cope flavored cotton candy.
What his story actually proves is that advantages are not invincibility shields.
A Ferrari can still be driven into a lake by a drunk driver.
The real clown move is taking an extreme outlier and treating it like a universal law.
"One Chad became a drug addict, therefore being attractive doesn't help."
Okay, and one billionaire lost everything in crypto. Does that mean wealth provides no benefits? One Olympic athlete became obese. Does that mean fitness is worthless?
Of course not.
The fact that someone squandered advantages doesn't erase the advantages.

If anything, the shocking part of the story is precisely because people expect someone with those advantages to do better than average. Nobody hears "handsome popular private-school kid became addicted to drugs and gambling" and thinks, "Yep, exactly the outcome I'd predict."
So no, the lesson isn't that success, looks, popularity, status, education, and opportunity are meaningless.
The lesson is that life is not deterministic.
Anyone using this story as proof that advantages don't matter is juggling statistics with oven mitts while wearing a clown nose and repeatedly riding their tricycle into the same wall.

62349924
>>62349860
Losing at life while playing on easy mode is really something else.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349929
>>62349886
lil chud really sat down and wrote a mini-essay that nobody is going to read

62349935
fellas, relax

im just saying in my head i imagined him being some super successful person now

we grew up playing together on the street, but eventually drifted a part as he became a chad and i became a chud

The funny thing is we probably have way more close of a relatable lifestyle now, sort of like how we did as kids

62349949
>>62349929
that's botslop

62349951
>>62349929
Chud used ChatGPT. Contains a couple of LLM syllogisms.

62349953
>>62349860
>>62349860
And WE are not addicted to online gambling? I lost 35% in Stocks and silver this year

62349965
>>62349953
no, WE are not

62349978
Which one Aron ? Idk anything beasidea parts of my story. Cheers lmao

62349987
Definitely didn’t read the mini book we just come here to laugh not read. Humor is the best medicine,

62350000
>>62349860
Why would a guys own mother say to her friend she hasn't seen in a long time that her son is a drug addict and a gambler? Ask yourself that, maybe this guy is so chad that he cut that bitch loose, all that parasitic weight on his shoulders, all the expectation, the nagging, the milestones which need to be hit for his mothers ego, the gaslighting and manipulation from his mother befriending his girlfriends and developing a seperate friendship with her where she can manipulate you through her turning your own wife into a version of your mother. Toxic family attatchments just dragging him the fuck down.

62350009
>>62349953
Thank you for your money.

62350016
>>62350000
Shitting on her son like that could be an indicator that she the real problem, but then it's equally true that women are retards in general and they just do random illogical shit. She could be some idiot NPC woman who loves her son but always rambles what's on her mind without ever thinking ahead. There's a lot of women like that.

62337937

62338026

>ICP has had many adoptions this year.

62343888

62343892

jerry did so many shrooms that he is taking out loans against his family and soul to buy the increasingly lower bottoms

62344871
i need it

62345073

62346259

62347287

item.title: A 30 y from 2022 with a face value of $1000 would roughly have a market value of $500 today. — 12 replies — 19.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
A 30 y from 2022 with a face value of $1000 would roughly have a market value of $500 today.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:28 UTC

OP

A 30 y from 2022 with a face value of $1000 would roughly have a market value of $500 today.
..and vice versa, should the yield go down, say we go back to 2% in 2030, then $1000 in a 5% bond would have a market value of ~$2000.

Say nothing happens, or the yield surge further.. You are still getting that 5% ROI on the face value, you don't have to sell, you can just enjoy a stable cashflow.

Early replies (first 3)

62349850
not buying your worthless dollars, mr. Bessent, you literal faggot.

62349858
I think this is a good investment, because from my perspective bond yields will go lower.
1) None of the inflation seems to be structural in any way, it's fuelled by active decisions - tariffs, Hormuz, widening of the budget deficit.
2) The economy really is bifurcated - this includes the labour market, and I think that a need will rise to adjust monetary policy to aid the lagging part of the economy, support the labour market.
3) An extension of 2, if the AI bubble pop, rates will go lower. You cut rates during a recession to stimulate the economy.
4) back to the 5% ROI.. If none of the above materialize, you are just stuck at a suboptimal investment, but 5% is not bad, especially because it is risk free.

62349906
I knew some dipshit that went all in on bonds in I think early 2023 because he thought stocks were topping and interest rates just had to go lower. Missed out on a lot of gains, but admittedly, the yield curve was inverted at the time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349916
rates will go higher this year, the impending oil crisis will see to that
don't buy long-term bonds until they hit at least 6%

62349921
>>62349858
>it's fuelled by active decisions
Yeah, surely it has nothing to do with compounding debt where the interest payments alone will soon make up the #1 largest expenditure and is very obviously spiraling out of control and everyone in charge are sticking their heads in the sand, lmao

62349954
>>62349921
Japan has a humongous debt, and it's interest rate is sub 1%.
..because what else could it be? no way they could ever make interest payment of 5%.
The US will probably follow the same track, cut interest rate, force retirement funds to buy these shit-yielding-bonds.

62349961
>>62349916
This is my thinking as well, i think 5% is interesting.. But we might get to 6-7%, think i will pull the trigger at these levels, close enough to the "10% average of the S&P", without any of the risk, and with the potential upside of x2.

62349974
>>62349961
with the added upside that treasuries are exempt from all but federal taxes

62349977
>>62349840
>say we go back to 2% in 2030
not gonna happen so no point in saying it

62349983
>>62349961
wouldn't go all in even at 6%, inflation will get really ugly in the next 30 years, you're better off diversifying

62350006
>>62349983
people hate inflation more than unemployment, that was made clear by the voters globally after COVID
I think we'll get a Volcker Shock 2.0 and then budget austerity well before inflation gets out of hand again

62350020
>>62349840
Trump is determined to increase inflation, therefore the bonds are sold because smart investors know, thus yields go up.

Hoping those shitty ass bonds will go up is the most hopium shit ass trade I ever heard.

item.title: Bitmine buys another 75000 ETH for $123M — 13 replies — 17.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitmine buys another 75000 ETH for $123M

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:20 UTC

OP

Is he crazy?

Early replies (first 3)

62349528
https://coincentral.com/tom-lee-linked-bitmine-adds-75000-eth-in-fresh-kraken-and-falconx-buying-spree/

62349535
TOP-SIGNAL

>gook scammer

62349565
>>62349522
>sell infinitely mintable shares to boomers and buy coins with profits
how is this a profitable strategy long term

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349712
>>62349522
1200 could be a nice bottom

62349716
eth isn't a long term earner but it is very volatile. if you're swing trading this is actually good. if you look at the chart there has been multiple times over the past few years to buy at 1000 and sell at 4000. youre a retard if you dont understand the opportunity there. i have 96 eth. going to leverage 50% once it breaks under 1400-ish. that will protect me on a drop or wick down to 800-900. i would bet 100k we see dont see it under 1000. sell the top. its easy money

62349727
>>62349716
Saar just buy the picobottom and sell the picotop benchod!

62349737
>>62349727

look at the chart you literal moron. it has hopped between 1-2k and 4-5k multiple times. do you really think you need higher than a room temperature iq to buy/sell in these ranges? here's a tip: it's in the buy range right now. it will go back up, even if it's to 2-3k, and you will make money. that is called swing trading. it's something people do when you have eyes and can see a recurring trend over many years. some of us aren't stupid and afraid and actually make money. you also need to realize these markets are made up. you can go on a jew casino exchange right now, borrow a margin loan at 5%, use a staked liquid eth token to borrow against it, then re-deposit the new eth you just bought as collateral against your margin loan. that would improve your liquidation protection to around 500. but you spend your days writing really unfunny pajeet jokes. im a white man btw go shit on a street while i make money. maybe take a hint

62349751
>>62349737
Yes saar, just time the market wo any error blody! Its ez doood!

62349759
>>62349751

youre going to stay poor man, i dont know what to tell you. eth isn't 4k right now. there's no 'timing the bottom', you buy it now, it will return to at least 2k at the literal worst, you sell, then repeat. crypto is not stocks. it is a volatile asset. i just went back, eth has gone from 1k range to 2-3k range 5 times since 2022. forget the leverage, you're right, you're not smart enough to understand risk management, just buy it and hold it until its high enough to sell. or don't, i know you won't, you're sit on here and get rugged on shitcoins and scam ai stocks and look at 'indicators' instead of using common sense. enjoy work tomorrow

62349771
>>62349759
At least u r already a gorillionaire from trading eth. Good 4u.

62349780
you have to be a complete retard to buy in right now
prices will fall dramatically because this new round of equity issuance is going to deplete the credit markets

62349797
>>62349780

my prediction is that it will go lower, yes, i'd guess around 1300 if btc falls to around 57k at a 2.3%-ish eth/btc ratio but you get into modeling the market which is just astrology for men. i am talking about common sense. the band we're in is a buy zone. this is only a dangerous buy zone if you're leveraging with bad risk management (morpho for example can fuck you on variable interest rates). buying at 1500 vs 1200 is not a life ending wealth destroying event. or even if it goes to 1k. its just not fully optimized. it will in time go back up in the 3-4k range and you sell. if you try to get too smart it ends up bottoming and you never buy. then you get nothing. on the off chance the retard like the op is right and it goes to 5-10k, then it's just cherry on the cupcake. eth is way more volatile than btc, it is possible to do this.

62350001
>>62349797
1200-1500 is my buy range

item.title: GIGA BLACK SWAN — 7 replies — 14.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
GIGA BLACK SWAN

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:37 UTC

OP

>>536838844
>>536838844
>>536838844
***WOOP WOOP WOOP***
***SUPRAECONOMIC DISASTER ENROUTE***
***PLEASE PROCEED TO EVACUATE CLEAN LAUNDRY TO BTC OR XRP OR SILVER***
***THIS IS NOT A DRILL***
***WOOOOOOOOOOP***

Early replies (first 3)

62349832
>>62349821
>BTC OR XRP OR SILVER
lmao bag shills, enjoy your 80% drop

62349833
Step right up, folks, gather ‘round the Big Top of Misunderstanding where the “Age of Expectancy Myth” is doing cartwheels in a burning barrel of outdated assumptions!
First act: “People used to all die at 30 in the olden days!”
Ohhh what a tragic little tale performed by the Clown of Bad Statistics! This joker looks at life expectancy at birth (which gets dragged down by infant mortality like a clown car sinking in a puddle) and screams, “EVERYONE WAS BASICALLY A TEENAGE GHOST!” No. If you survived childhood—which many did—you often made it into your 50s, 60s, even 70s. The circus trick here is ignoring survival curves and pretending averages are destiny. That’s like saying because one clown slips on a banana peel, the whole parade is doomed.

Second act: “Modern people only live longer because we’re superhuman now!”
Nope! No laser-eyed immortality upgrade. The real magic is boring but powerful: sanitation, vaccines, antibiotics, safer childbirth, and less infectious disease roulette. It’s not that humans suddenly became elite boss-level beings; it’s that we removed a lot of the instant-death landmines from the path.

Third act: “Aging itself has changed dramatically in biology!”
Careful now, this clown is juggling metaphors without looking. While medicine has improved healthspan (fewer years crippled by disease), the basic biological aging process didn’t suddenly get rewritten like a circus script mid-show. Bodies still age. Cells still misbehave. Gravity still wins.

Final act: “Everyone today is guaranteed to live super long!”
Ah yes, the grand finale where someone mistakes averages for promises. Life expectancy is not a prophecy etched into the cosmic popcorn machine.

So let the ringmaster declare: the myth that humans “barely lived past 30” is not history—it’s a juggling error with bad data and dramatic flair.

62349835
>>62349833
dirty slopper

>>62349821
>strategists led by Savita Subramanian
oh dear

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349842
>>62349821
In the related yahoo spam article, it lists "consumer confidence" > 100, not triggered. Why would higher confidence be a bear indicator now? Is that just jeetsloppa, or is that machine economy already, that humans need to be desperate for the economy to thrive?

62349859
>>62349821

>>62349856

62349971
>>62349859
spamming into a spam thread is pretty low you naughty clanker

62350043
Wow desu ne, all that wasted power, Bank of America.

Eh, they'll just turn this shit up to 11 for the sake of it because Bank of America already lost.

item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:03 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62348177
>>62348037
nintendo is dead. the new pokemon games all have a super weird art style

62348263
>>62348170
I couldn't tell, because I stopped paying attention (and let it die i guess)

Is there anything these days like Minecraft Halloween update, the summer of dayz, or starceaft brood war Use Map Settings maps?

62348278
>>62348037

Are you the same retard that asked about buying them after an entire thread told you they were cooked? I remember giving clear examples of why this company is dogshit, specifically not owning Pokémon 100%. They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son. Sounds like you are a braindead tendie lie the rest.

62349809
>>62348099
I would have lost more if I hadn't sold, retard.

62349911
>>62348037
I FUCKING told end of last year that Nintendo was in a bubble. Yes, it's in the archives, likely with either Xenoblade pics or Rosalina pics.
>Muh P/E is useless
It isn't. As many retards will soon learn again for lot of shit they buy at overpriced prices.
Now it's at a more reasonable price, still elevated, but low enough that I'm regularly buying.

62349918
>>62349911
nintebdo movies will each make a bil

62349956
the next ubisoft

62349957
>>62348037
you could have made 100k scalping pokemon cards, wtf are you doing OP

62349964
>>62348037
Japan as a society is super conservative and stuck in its way to a retarded degree, what do you expect from the land of fax machines and archipelago syndrome?

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-11 09:01 UTC

OP

Even Kraken (wtf do they have to do with stonks?) sent me a message 'dont miss SpaceX IPO!!'.
I cant believe people are so stupid that they will buy in mass and get dumped on. So if everybody is expecting this scenario to happen, what is really going to happen?

Early replies (first 3)

62350036
>>62349984
kek investing and the stock market are the most fucked up psychological torture game

its gonna go up and then dump

62350091
>>62349984
It will go up massively. But therefore it cannot survive the next market downturn. Which might be just around the corner, it seems. Too much hype the last three years.

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 4 replies — 6.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-11 08:27 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350015
>>62349960
Eh. I think they'll just turn shit up to 11 because Bank of America lost.

62345958

62345960
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62345970
most of the so called utility coins have been around for 14-15 years, if they were actually going to do something they would have 10 times already. Even the fucking dotcom boom crashed and recovered in half the time. What else is there to wait for? Clarity Act? Don't make me laugh.

62346026
>>62345970
You could have held Walmart and apple for 20 years before it really took off, 12 years for Amazon…so you’re a retard

62346045

62346051

62347125
>>62346045
I’ve been holding XRP for years, but no single good news has pumped its prices. Even when XRP got clarity in court, its price only went up measly 40 cents before it dumped back down. You can declare XRP intergalactic bridge currency, but it will still dump or crab. This is because they’re using bots to suppress the price, which has been documented for a long time.

>>62347125

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 190 replies — 27.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 190 • Updated: 2026-06-11 02:03 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337475
If chainlink is decentralized why do not even the biggest, stinkiest linkies here brag about running a node?

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

62344923
>>62342602
All of Chainlink's tokens go through Binance, including node payments.
Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

62346060
>>62342559
>He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.


not sure why you keep repeating this as if this implies some transparency.

>duuuuuuuuuude just hang out on the blockchain every friday and you'll see it coming

its ok to concede they don't want to be transparent here. if they did they would just post a set schedule.

>>62344923
>Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

no one falls for this outside of the biggest retard boyscouts
where do the coins go?

62346092
>>62346060
Unlocking the same amount annually for years, within the same interval for years, and even around the same time of day for years implies transparency, yes.

>where do the coins go?
among others to the nodes

62346134
link blowz

62349168
Posting in the best LINK thread now. Some good convos. I hope we all make it.
